commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=3cdf3b0a21f0d38de97e9939c07c5222f9bead0c)
commands.review_summary:
The branch preserves legacy Codex model/runtime routes in non-interactive doctor/update flows, adds protected-route rollback/fail-closed handling, and surfaces cross-provider fallback state.

PR surface: Source +293, Tests +169. Total +462 across 11 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source and the linked live incident show current main's non-interactive doctor repair can rewrite legacy Codex refs; this read-only review did not execute the CLI.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human follow-up is needed for the P1 audit fix, docs alignment, redacted update/finalize proof, and base refresh before merge.

target.title: fix(update): preserve protected Codex routes
target.body:
## Summary

- Preserve legacy `openai-codex/*` and `codex/*` protected model/runtime routes during non-interactive doctor repair instead of silently rewriting them to `openai/*`.
- Add update-time protected-route audit/rollback for core update and post-core finalization: if a protected Codex route is rewritten, restore the pre-update config and fail closed with `protected-route-drift`.
- Mark cross-provider/auth-route-changing fallbacks in fallback telemetry and keep route-changing fallback notices visible even when upstream verbose fallback output is off.
- Keep interactive `openclaw doctor --fix` migration behavior unchanged.

## Evidence

This is based on the live incident captured in #80628 and comment https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/80628#issuecomment-4585716866.

Observed on OpenClaw 2026.5.28:

- `openclaw doctor` touched `/root/.openclaw/openclaw.json` at `2026-05-31T04:29:14Z`.
- A previously working Codex setup had model refs rewritten from a Codex route to OpenAI-provider refs across `agents.defaults.model.primary` and specialist-agent `model.primary` / `fallbacks` entries.
- The next isolated `News Digest` cron run failed before reply under the Codex harness with:

```text
Requested agent harness "codex" does not support openai/gpt-5.5 (provider is not one of: codex).
```

Local remediation was to patch the config back to Codex model refs, remove `openai/gpt-5.5` from the allowed agent model sets, and let the gateway hot-reload. After remediation, `openclaw models status --json` reported `defaultModel` and `resolvedDefault` as `codex/gpt-5.5` with allowed `codex/gpt-5.5` and `xai/grok-4.3`.

This PR now covers the full failure mode from #80628: doctor preservation, update audit/rollback, and visibility for cross-provider fallback transitions.

Refs #80628, #88450, #88470.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Non-interactive repair and update flows no longer silently rewrite protected Codex route model refs to OpenAI-provider refs.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on this PR branch, Node v22.22.2, pnpm 11.2.2, isolated temporary `OPENCLAW_HOME` and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` with no production config or credentials.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Created a temporary config containing `agents.defaults.model: "openai-codex/gpt-5.5"`, then ran `HOME="$tmpdir" OPENCLAW_HOME="$tmpdir" OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$config" timeout 90s pnpm openclaw doctor --non-interactive --fix`.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal capture from the patched branch:

```text
doctor exit=0
config after doctor:
{
  "model": "openai-codex/gpt-5.5"
}

preservation warning lines:
43:│  - Legacy `openai-codex/*` model refs should be rewritten to            │
49:│  - Preserved legacy `openai-codex/*` model refs during non-interactive  │

last output lines:
Updated config: $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/openclaw.json
  Backup: $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/openclaw.json.bak
Backup: $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/openclaw.json.bak
│
└  Doctor complete.
```

- Observed result after fix: Doctor completed and wrote the isolated config while leaving the protected model route as `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`; the new preservation warning was emitted in the doctor output.
- Additional guarded behavior: Update tests now simulate protected route drift from `codex/gpt-5.5` to `openai/gpt-5.5` and assert the update restores the pre-update config, emits `protected-route-drift`, exits non-zero, and skips post-update plugin work.
- What was not tested: A live production gateway restart on the patched branch was not run; this proof only exercises the real doctor CLI against an isolated local OpenClaw config.

## Tests

- `pnpm tsgo:test:src`
- `git diff --check`
- `env -u OPENCLAW_SERVICE_MARKER -u OPENCLAW_SERVICE_KIND node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/cli/update-cli.test.ts src/auto-reply/fallback-state.test.ts src/agents/model-fallback.probe.test.ts src/commands/doctor/repair-sequencing.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/codex-route-warnings.test.ts src/commands/doctor-config-flow.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner.sanitize-session-history.test.ts src/infra/device-auth-store.test.ts`
- Final post-rebase smoke: `env -u OPENCLAW_SERVICE_MARKER -u OPENCLAW_SERVICE_KIND node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/cli/update-cli.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner.sanitize-session-history.test.ts`
target.author: pfrederiksen

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
